page_title: Paid family and sick leave — Minnesota

seo.title: Paid Family and Sick Leave — Minnesota 2026 | Teamed
seo.meta_description: Minnesota PFML went live 1 January 2026: 0.88% premium, up to 20 combined weeks per year. ESST: 1 hour per 30 worked, 48-hour cap. Employer compliance guide.
secondary_keywords: Minnesota earned sick and safe time
MN paid family medical leave premium rate

hero_image.alt_text: Father carrying young child on his shoulders outdoors — illustrating family leave and parental bonding time in Minnesota.

pfml.programme_name: Minnesota Paid Family and Medical Leave (MN PFML)

esst.programme_name: Minnesota Earned Sick and Safe Time (ESST)

prompt_corrections.note: ROW spec references '0.66% depending on opt-out election'. Cache data specifies 75% of 0.88% = 0.66% for employers with ≤30 employees meeting average-wage condition. The phrase 'opt-out election' in the ROW may refer to a private plan election, not a different rate mechanism. Verify precise statutory language at paidleave.mn.gov before publish.
prompt_corrections.verified_value: 75% of standard rate = ~0.66% for qualifying small employers
prompt_corrections.severity: low — rate figure is correct; terminology precision requires review

factual_uncertainties.note: The mechanism described as 'opt-out election' in the ROW spec is represented as the small-employer size + wage-condition exemption in the compliance cache. Both converge on 0.66% but the statutory trigger language should be verified at paidleave.mn.gov before publish.

pullquote_provenance.source: Teamed voice — no verbatim Meeting Intelligence quote used. Anonymisation not required; attribution is to role/company not a specific MI transcript.
